subject: Language Arts
sample_standards: Your child understands how regions help organize and describe Earth's surface.
Your child uses tools to identify, describe, and draw special four-sided shapes and sorts flat shapes based on their features.
Your child can observe and describe shapes by their important features, sort and draw them, and begin learning about fractions by dividing shapes.
Your child uses tools to explore the features of triangles and circles.
Your child can locate all the continents on a map or globe.
Your child understands how Gestalt ideas help us see and understand things as whole patterns.
Your child can find important lines on maps and globes like the prime meridian, equator, Tropics of Capricorn and Cancer, International Date Line, and lines of latitude and longitude.
Your child learns about shapes by looking at their parts and how they fit together, and practices dividing shapes to understand fractions.
canonical_name: Grade 4 Math: Geometry — Attributes and Classification of Shapes
coherence_reason: The standards include both geometry concepts (attributes and classification of shapes, fractions of shapes) and geography concepts (regions, continents, map features), so the grouping is not coherent.
split_topics: Geometry: Attributes and Classification of Shapes
Geography: Regions, Continents, and Map Features

subject: Language Arts
sample_standards: Your child can explain how industrial growth, city life, new communication, and cultural changes affected life in the U.S. from the late 1800s to World War I.
Your child can explain what caused World War I, what happened because of it, and why the United States joined the war.
Your child can describe how people worked to improve society and change unfair conditions during the Progressive Era.
Your child studies and compares how Indiana's economy has changed from the past to today.
Your child understands how American democracy began and its main ideas.
Your child understands how World War I affected people in Alabama, including African Americans moving north and west, use of military bases, and making more goods for the war.
Your child understands why Europeans explored and settled in Alabama and how this affected trade, health, and land.
Your child can name important events and celebrations that happen in your town and in Alabama.
canonical_name: Grade 4 Social Studies: United States History — Industrialization, Progressive Era, and World War I
coherence_reason: The standards reference multiple broad topics: industrial growth, World War I, the Progressive Era, European exploration, local and state events, and democracy, which are loosely related but span different historical periods and concepts.
split_topics: Industrial growth, city life, communication, and cultural changes (late 1800s–WWI)
Causes and effects of World War I
Origins and ideas of American democracy
Effects of World War I in Alabama
European exploration and its impact on Alabama
Local and state events in Alabama

subject: Language Arts
sample_standards: Your child will find the main idea of a text, explain how details support it, and summarize the text.
Your child understands and uses correct grammar and word usage when writing or speaking.
Your child can find the opposite meaning of a word they see in a text.
Your child can find or list words, facts, or details that are connected to a topic.
Your child can use letter sounds to read words.
Your child can pick a word or picture that shows their opinion about a topic or story.
By the end of fourth grade, your child can read and understand stories, plays, and poems at their grade level, with some help for harder parts.
Your child can tell when to use formal language (like talking to a teacher) and when to use informal language (like talking to a friend).
canonical_name: Grade 4 Language Arts: Reading Comprehension — Main Idea and Supporting Details
coherence_reason: The standards cover a wide range of topics including main idea, grammar, vocabulary, phonics, opinion, reading comprehension, and language register, which are all distinct subtopics in Language Arts.
split_topics: Reading Comprehension (main idea, details, summarizing, grade-level texts)
Grammar and Usage (grammar, word usage, formal/informal language)
Opinion (expressing opinions about a topic or story)

subject: Language Arts
sample_standards: Your child can use math skills to figure out the cost of borrowing money.
Your child can compare the good and bad points of different credit products and services.
Your child knows that contracts are promises people must keep.
Your child understands that people decide how to spend money based on what they have, what they need, and what they want.
Your child can name products people use at banks and other financial places.
Your child can compare different financial services and products to see which fit their goals.
Your child can explain where to find help and protection as a consumer, including public groups and private experts or websites.
Your child can explore how business methods, like sales techniques, affect consumers.
canonical_name: Grade 4 Language Arts: Financial Literacy — Consumer Awareness and Financial Decision-Making
coherence_reason: All standards focus on financial literacy, consumer awareness, and decision-making, though some emphasize contracts and borrowing while others focus on choosing financial products.
